title: cymbidium palustre
intro: Cymbidium palustre, also known as the marsh cymbidium, is a species of orchid that grows in wetland areas and is characterized by its long, narrow leaves and showy white flowers. This plant is used in traditional medicine due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which can be attributed to its flavonoids and terpenoids content. The health benefits of Cymbidium palustre include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and protect against certain infections. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and flowers are often dried and used to make teas or infusions, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
